content.claim: Finland's 2010–2011 education reform introduced a three-tiered support system (general, intensified, special) as a direct response to rising special education identification, restructuring provision from a two-tier to a three-tier non-categorical model.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Finnish system reform lens
limitations: Based on institutional reform records rather than peer-reviewed outcome studies
Reform design rationale does not establish whether rising identification reflected underlying need or administrative/incentive factors
No direct causal evidence that the three-tier redesign reduced overall identification rates
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
